front: War: fought 1700-1721 between Charles XII's Sweden and a coalition led by Peter the Great's Russia, joined by Denmark and Poland-Saxony
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: War: ended with the 1721 Treaty of Nystad and marked Russia's ascendancy as the dominant Baltic power over Sweden
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Event: forced Augustus the Strong to renounce the Polish throne after the Battle of Fraustadt
work: Causes, Early Campaigns & Augustus the Strong's Ouster

front: Ruler: charged with treason for defecting during the Great Northern War and executed by being broken on the wheel
work: Causes, Early Campaigns & Augustus the Strong's Ouster

front: Event: Sweden's defeat cost it the Sound Dues exemption it had won in the earlier Torstenson War
work: Causes, Early Campaigns & Augustus the Strong's Ouster

front: Event: Sweden's defeat cost it the provinces of Ingria and Livonia
work: Causes, Early Campaigns & Augustus the Strong's Ouster

front: Ruler: while negotiating the anti-Swedish alliance, competed with Peter the Great over who could bend more silverware with bare hands
work: Causes, Early Campaigns & Augustus the Strong's Ouster

front: Event: early in the war, the elite Drabant Corps used a smoke screen while crossing the Duna River to attack Saxon forces
work: Causes, Early Campaigns & Augustus the Strong's Ouster

front: Event: a plot by ambassadors Erik Sparre and Karl Gyllenborg led to British intervention in the war
work: Causes, Early Campaigns & Augustus the Strong's Ouster

front: Battle: Charles XII's Swedish army won a surprise victory in a blizzard here, relieving a Russian siege of the city
work: Battle of Narva

front: Battle: the war's decisive turning point (1709), in which Peter the Great's army crushed Charles XII's forces
work: Battle of Poltava

front: Figure: this Cossack hetman defected to the Swedish side at Poltava after learning Peter the Great intended to replace him with Alexander Menshikov
work: Battle of Poltava

front: Figure: fled with the defeated Swedes to the Ottoman Empire after Poltava, where he died
work: Battle of Poltava

front: Event: Russian forces defeated a supply column led by General Adam Lewenhaupt here shortly before Poltava, depriving Charles XII's army of reinforcements
work: Battle of Poltava

front: Battle: the Georgian prince Alexander of Imereti was captured here
work: Battle of Poltava

front: Event: this battle was the subject of a painting by Alexander von Kotzebue depicting soldiers retreating across a snow-covered river
work: Battle of Poltava

front: Event: took refuge at the Ottoman town of Bender in Moldova, where his entourage's lavish spending earned him a nickname meaning "Iron-Head"
work: Charles XII's Ottoman Exile & the Pruth River Campaign

front: Event: an Ottoman operation at Bender known as the "kalabalik" briefly captured/imprisoned this Swedish king
work: Charles XII's Ottoman Exile & the Pruth River Campaign

front: Event: Peter the Great launched this campaign, allied with Prince Dmitrie Cantemir, after the Ottomans refused to surrender Charles XII
work: Charles XII's Ottoman Exile & the Pruth River Campaign

front: Event: during this campaign, Peter the Great's forces were surrounded by Grand Vizier Baltadji at the Battle of Stanilesti but received surprisingly lenient peace terms
work: Charles XII's Ottoman Exile & the Pruth River Campaign

front: Battle: this 1714 Russian naval victory near the Hanko Peninsula was led by Admiral Fyodor Apraksin
work: Battle of Gangut

front: Battle: the Swedish flagship Elefanten was captured here and its admiral taken prisoner after refusing to surrender
work: Battle of Gangut

front: Battle: the Russian navy has always kept a ship named for this battle ever since, in commemoration
work: Battle of Gangut

front: Battle: Swedish musketeers used "Ga-Pa" tactics (marching to fifty paces, then charging to twenty meters for a volley and bayonet charge) to defeat Jacob von Flemming's cavalry here
work: Battle of Gadebusch & the Carolean Death March

front: Event: the victorious Swedish army from Gadebusch was later decimated retreating through Norway's Tydal mountains in an action remembered by this name
work: Battle of Gadebusch & the Carolean Death March

front: Event: hundreds of civilians were massacred in this incident, part of the Russian occupation of Finland known as the Great Wrath
work: The Great Wrath

front: Event: refugees displaced by the war spread a plague outbreak that coincided with the most extreme of the era's "Great Frosts"
work: The Great Wrath

front: Event: Charles XII was shot in the head and killed here, years after fleeing to Ottoman Moldavia
work: End of the War: Death of Charles XII & Treaty of Nystad

front: Event: the site of Charles XII's death, marked by a cross of two peat strips, opens a cultural history written by this historian
work: End of the War: Death of Charles XII & Treaty of Nystad

front: Event: this 1721 treaty ended the Great Northern War, transferring Ingria and Karelia to Russia while returning most of Finland to Sweden
work: End of the War: Death of Charles XII & Treaty of Nystad
